verses.text: Beaten by the Philistines, Israel does not repent but fetches the ark into camp, as if the box could save what their sin had forfeited. They treat God's presence as a lucky charm. Faith obeys God; superstition merely tries to use Him.

Beaten by the Philistines, Israel does not repent but fetches the ark into camp, as if the box could save what their sin had forfeited. They treat God's presence as a lucky charm. Faith obeys God; superstition merely tries to use Him.

Israel raises a great shout, and the Philistines tremble at the news — yet resolve to fight. A loud noise about God is no substitute for walking with Him. The ark cannot rescue a people who keep the symbol but despise the LORD it represents.

Israel raises a great shout, and the Philistines tremble at the news — yet resolve to fight. A loud noise about God is no substitute for walking with Him. The ark cannot rescue a people who keep the symbol but despise the LORD it represents.

The disaster falls: Israel is routed, the ark captured, and Eli's sons Hophni and Phinehas die in one day, exactly as God had said. The presence they presumed to command was taken from them. God will not be carried about by those who will not bow.

The disaster falls: Israel is routed, the ark captured, and Eli's sons Hophni and Phinehas die in one day, exactly as God had said. The presence they presumed to command was taken from them. God will not be carried about by those who will not bow.

Old, blind Eli sits by the road, his heart 'trembling for the ark of God.' For all his weakness, his last anxiety is for the things of God, not merely for his sons. He dreads above everything that God's glory has been lost.

Old, blind Eli sits by the road, his heart 'trembling for the ark of God.' For all his weakness, his last anxiety is for the things of God, not merely for his sons. He dreads above everything that God's glory has been lost.

The messenger tells it all — Israel fled, both sons dead, the ark taken — and at the word 'ark' Eli falls backward and dies. The judgment long foretold arrives in a moment. The man who would not restrain his sons outlives them by only an hour.

The messenger tells it all — Israel fled, both sons dead, the ark taken — and at the word 'ark' Eli falls backward and dies. The judgment long foretold arrives in a moment. The man who would not restrain his sons outlives them by only an hour.

The messenger tells it all — Israel fled, both sons dead, the ark taken — and at the word 'ark' Eli falls backward and dies. The judgment long foretold arrives in a moment. The man who would not restrain his sons outlives them by only an hour.

Phinehas' widow, dying in childbirth, names her son Ichabod — 'the glory is departed from Israel' — for the ark of God was taken. It is the saddest name in Scripture. When God's presence is presumed upon and lost, no other loss compares; nothing matters once the glory is gone.
